content.text: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 is the first internationally recognised management-system standard specifically for artificial intelligence. It specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an AI Management System (AIMS) — a structured governance framework that spans the entire AI lifecycle from planning through decommissioning. Unlike ISO 27001 (information security) or SOC 2, ISO 42001 addresses AI-specific risks: bias, transparency, human oversight, and societal impact.

: it applies to any organisation that develops or deploys AI systems and is structured using the ISO High Level Structure (HLS), making it compatible with ISO 27001 and ISO 9001. Organisations with an existing ISO 27001 ISMS can integrate an AIMS with significant clause overlap reuse.

attrs.alt: ISO 42001 AIMS Plan-Do-Check-Act for GenAI diagram

content.text: ISO 42001 distinguishes two organisational roles with different obligations:
Develops or makes available an AI system (including fine-tuned models, internal platforms)
Full Annex A control set; model card; risk assessment; training data governance
Uses a third-party AI system in their own products or workflows
Use-case risk assessment; TPRM for AI vendor; user disclosure; HITL where needed
Uses third-party LLM but wraps it in a proprietary application sold to customers
Obligations of both roles apply; scope must be explicitly defined in the AIMS

using API-based LLMs from third-party providers. The Deployer obligations are less extensive than Provider obligations but still material — particularly the use-case risk assessment (Clause 6.1) and AI impact assessment (Annex A.4.2).

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from datetime import date
from typing import Optional

class AISystemRole(str, Enum):
    PROVIDER  = "provider"    # develops/deploys the AI system
    DEPLOYER  = "deployer"    # uses a third-party AI system in their products
    BOTH      = "both"

class AILifecycleStage(str, Enum):
    CONCEPTION  = "conception"
    DESIGN      = "design"
    DEVELOPMENT = "development"
    VALIDATION  = "validation"
    DEPLOYMENT  = "deployment"
    OPERATION   = "operation"
    DECOMMISSION = "decommission"

@dataclass
class AIUseCase:
    """
    ISO 42001 Clause 8.4 requires documenting each AI system with its
    intended purpose, risk classification, and lifecycle stage.
    """
    use_case_id: str
    name: str
    description: str
    role: AISystemRole
    high_risk: bool                 # ISO 42001 / EU AI Act high-risk classification
    lifecycle_stage: AILifecycleStage
    owner: str                      # accountable AI system owner
    risk_assessment_ref: str        # link to the risk assessment in the register
    model_card_ref: Optional[str] = None
    last_reviewed: Optional[date] = None

@dataclass
class AIPolicy:
    """
    ISO 42001 Clause 5.2 requires a documented AI policy approved by top management.
    """
    version: str
    approved_by: str
    approval_date: date
    scope: str                   # which AI systems are covered
    principles: list[str]        # e.g. transparency, fairness, accountability
    objectives: list[str]        # measurable AI objectives (Clause 6.2)
    review_cycle_months: int = 12

    def is_current(self) -> bool:
        months_old = (date.today() - self.approval_date).days / 30
        return months_old <= self.review_cycle_months

@dataclass
class ISO42001RiskAssessment:
    """
    ISO 42001 Clause 6.1 requires a documented risk assessment methodology
    and assessed risks for each AI use case.
    """
    use_case_id: str
    assessment_date: date
    assessor: str
    methodology: str         # e.g. "ISO 42001 Annex A + NIST AI RMF"

    # Identified risks (maps to enterprise risk register)
    risks: list[dict]        # [{risk_id, description, likelihood, impact, controls}]

    # ISO 42001-specific impact categories
    impacts_on_individuals: list[str]  # discrimination, privacy, autonomy
    impacts_on_groups: list[str]       # systemic bias, disproportionate effect
    impacts_on_society: list[str]      # labour market, environment, democracy
    impacts_on_organisation: list[str] # reputational, legal, operational

    approval_status: str = "pending"   # "pending" | "approved" | "rejected"
    approved_by: Optional[str] = None
    next_review: Optional[date] = None

    def has_unmitigated_high_risks(self) -> bool:
        return any(
            r.get("residual_risk_level") == "high"
            for r in self.risks
        )

ISO 42001 Annex A provides a reference set of AI-specific controls. Unlike ISO 27001 Annex A, it is not exhaustive — organisations select and implement controls based on their risk assessment. The most operationally relevant controls for GenAI deployers are:
ISO 42001 requires at least an annual management review. The review inputs and outputs must be documented and retained as evidence:

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime

@dataclass
class ManagementReviewInput:
    """ISO 42001 Clause 9.3 — inputs the management review must consider."""
    review_date: datetime
    previous_actions_status: list[dict]   # [{action, owner, due, status}]
    kri_dashboard_snapshot: dict          # {kri_name: {value, threshold, status}}
    internal_audit_findings: list[str]
    nonconformities: list[str]            # Clause 10.1 items
    risk_register_changes: list[str]
    stakeholder_feedback: list[str]       # complaints, user feedback, regulator comms
    ai_policy_current: bool               # Clause 5.2 currency check

@dataclass
class ManagementReviewOutput:
    """ISO 42001 Clause 9.3 — decisions and actions from the review."""
    review_date: datetime
    opportunities_for_improvement: list[str]
    resource_changes: list[str]         # additional budget, headcount, tooling
    aims_effectiveness_assessment: str  # "effective" | "partially effective" | "ineffective"
    actions: list[dict]                 # [{action, owner, target_date}]
    next_review_date: datetime
    approved_by: str

    def to_evidence_record(self) -> dict:
        """Convert to a dict suitable for audit evidence archival."""
        return {
            "type": "management_review",
            "review_date": self.review_date.isoformat(),
            "effectiveness": self.aims_effectiveness_assessment,
            "action_count": len(self.actions),
            "approved_by": self.approved_by,
            "next_review": self.next_review_date.isoformat(),
        }
Map regulatory environment (EU AI Act, GDPR), competitive landscape, and internal AI maturity
Regulators, customers, employees affected by AI, AI vendors, civil society
Board-approved policy covering transparency, fairness, human oversight, and accountability principles
AI policy v1.x with approval signature
Use-case-level risk assessment covering individual, group, societal, and organisational impacts
Risk assessment per use case; risk register
SMART objectives: bias rate < 2%, HITL review rate > 95% for high-stakes, model uptime > 99.5%
Objectives register with owners and KPIs
Training plan for AI practitioners; roles with AI accountability have AI ethics module in onboarding
Formal lifecycle stages with defined entry/exit criteria and owner sign-off at each gate
Lifecycle gate checklist per use case
KRI dashboard; control test results; bias evaluation cadence defined in policy
Annual AIMS internal audit by independent function (Risk/Audit team)
Annual review with board/CISO; inputs include audit findings, KRIs, stakeholder feedback
Corrective action process for AIMS failures; link to finding remediation tracker
Organisations with an existing ISO 27001 ISMS can integrate ISO 42001 by:

content: : add AI systems to the ISMS scope, or define a separate but co-located AIMS scope.
: ISO 27001 Clause 6.1 risk assessment methodology can be extended to cover AI-specific impact dimensions (individual, societal) required by ISO 42001.
: a single annual management review can satisfy both standards if both sets of inputs are addressed and minuted.
: maintain one control library with controls tagged for ISO 27001 Annex A, ISO 42001 Annex A, and SOC 2 TSC. Avoid three separate spreadsheets.
: the internal audit function can cover both ISMS and AIMS in the same annual cycle, with AI-specific expertise supplementing the IT audit team.

content.text:
def combined_framework_tags(control_id: str, control_library: dict) -> dict:
    """
    Returns the framework citations for a given control across
    ISO 42001, ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST AI RMF.
    """
    ctrl = control_library.get(control_id, {})
    return {
        "control_id":   control_id,
        "iso_42001":    ctrl.get("iso_42001_refs", []),   # e.g. ["A.6.1", "A.6.2"]
        "iso_27001":    ctrl.get("iso_27001_refs", []),   # e.g. ["A.8.1", "A.8.2"]
        "soc2_tsc":     ctrl.get("soc2_refs", []),        # e.g. ["CC6.1", "C1.2"]
        "nist_ai_rmf":  ctrl.get("nist_ai_rmf_refs", []), # e.g. ["GOVERN-1.1", "MAP-1.5"]
    }
ISO 42001 certification requires a certification body audit (Stage 1: document review; Stage 2: effectiveness audit). The most commonly requested evidence:
Signed and dated; version controlled; communicated to all staff
Every in-scope AI system listed with role, lifecycle stage, risk level, and owner
One assessment per use case; documented methodology; approval records
For high-risk use cases; DPO or ethics officer sign-off
For each production model: intended use, limitations, bias evaluation results
Operational effectiveness evidence for Annex A controls in scope
Findings, ratings, and corrective actions from past 12 months
Inputs addressed; improvement decisions documented; action log
Root cause, corrective action, verification of closure

content: : organisations often have dozens of AI tools deployed by individual teams with no central inventory. Clause 4.1 requires you to know what AI systems are in scope.

content.text: Risk assessment treats AI like IT risk

content: : ISO 42001 requires impact assessment across individual, group, societal, and organisational dimensions — not just confidentiality/integrity/availability. IT risk teams often miss the societal dimension entirely.

content.text: AI policy approved but not communicated

content: : Clause 5.2 requires the policy to be communicated to all relevant personnel. A policy in a SharePoint folder that staff have not read does not satisfy the requirement.

content.text: No model card for production models

content: : Annex A.8.5 (transparency) is frequently partially implemented — confidence scores may exist in logs but model cards documenting limitations and bias evaluation results are missing.

: organisations that absorb ISO 42001 into an existing ISO 27001 programme often find the AI-specific inputs (AI incidents, bias evaluation results, AI policy currency) were not addressed in the combined management review.

content.text: ISO 42001 provides a structured management system framework for AI governance built on the familiar Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle. For GenAI deployers, the critical implementation steps are: maintain a complete use-case inventory, conduct a risk assessment for each use case with ISO 42001's four impact dimensions, publish a board-approved AI policy, implement the relevant Annex A controls (especially A.6.1 data governance, A.7.3 testing, and A.8.5 transparency), and conduct an annual management review that specifically addresses AI-system performance, incidents, and policy currency. Organisations with ISO 27001 can integrate ISO 42001 by extending their existing risk methodology, control library, and audit programme rather than building a separate system.
